## Cloudflare Workers (via @cloudflare/vite-plugin)

This adapter integrates Qwik City with [Cloudflare Workers](https://workers.cloudflare.com/) using the official [`@cloudflare/vite-plugin`](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/vite-plugin/). It provides accurate emulation of the Workers environment during development via the [workerd](https://github.com/cloudflare/workerd) runtime.

### Development

Start the local development server with workerd emulation:

```
npm run dev
```

Then visit [http://localhost:5173/](http://localhost:5173/)

The development server uses `@cloudflare/vite-plugin` to run your SSR code in an actual `workerd` instance, giving you access to Cloudflare Bindings (vars, Secrets, KV, D1, R2, etc.) during development.

### Production Build

Build for Cloudflare Workers:

```
npm run build
```

This runs two steps:
1. `vite build` — generates the client bundle in `dist/`
2. `vite build -c adapters/cloudflare-worker/vite.config.ts` — generates the Worker bundle in `dist/build/` and a stable entry at `server/entry.worker.mjs`

### Deployments

Deploy to Cloudflare Workers:

```
npm run deploy
```

If you don't already have an account, [create a Cloudflare account here](https://dash.cloudflare.com/sign-up/workers-and-pages).

### Configuration

The `wrangler.jsonc` file contains your Worker configuration. Key settings include:

- **name**: Your Worker's name
- **main**: `./server/entry.worker.mjs` (stable entry generated by the build)
- **compatibility_date**: The date used for compatibility with the Workers runtime
- **assets**: Serves static files (JS, CSS, images) automatically before the Worker is invoked
- **bindings**: Resources your Worker can access (KV, D1, R2, Secrets, etc.)

After adding any binding, regenerate the TypeScript types:

```
npm run cf-typegen
```

### Accessing Cloudflare Bindings

In route loaders, actions, and middleware, access Bindings via `requestEvent.platform.env`:

```typescript
export const useLoader = routeLoader$(async (requestEvent) => {
  const { env } = requestEvent.platform;
  // env.MY_KV — KV Namespace
  // env.MY_DB — D1 Database
  // env.MY_VAR — environment variable (from wrangler.jsonc vars)
});
```

For TypeScript types, add your bindings to `worker-configuration.d.ts` (or run `npm run cf-typegen`):

```typescript
interface Env {
  ASSETS: Fetcher;
  MY_VAR: string;
  MY_KV: KVNamespace;
}
```

### Local Bindings (Development)

To use Bindings in the local dev server, configure them in `.dev.vars` (for vars/secrets) or `wrangler.jsonc` (for KV, D1, etc.). The `.dev.vars` file is not committed to git.

For more details, see the [Wrangler configuration documentation](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/wrangler/configuration/).
